title: Concurrency versus parallelism deterministic draft bank

publication.changeSummary: Misconception MCQ, canonical fill-gap, and no code task for Concurrency versus parallelism; every item remains blocked from formal exams pending independent human review.

items.title: Concurrency versus parallelism: reject the misconception
items.prompt: Which statement is source-aligned for Python: Beginner to Intermediate and corrects this misconception? Concurrency and parallelism mean the same thing, and adding threads always makes any Python workload faster.

examEligibility.rationale: AI-assisted draft item; independent human source, technical, pedagogy, accessibility, and answer-oracle review is required before formal-exam eligibility.

hints: Compare each option with both the language-specific boundary case and the cited operational model.

feedback.correct: Correct. Classify waiting versus computation and the desired latency or throughput; choose concurrency for overlapping progress and parallel resources only when simultaneous work offsets coordination cost.
feedback.incorrect: That choice preserves the misconception. Classify waiting versus computation and the desired latency or throughput; choose concurrency for overlapping progress and parallel resources only when simultaneous work offsets coordination cost.

criteria.description: Selects the source-aligned correction and rejects the documented misconception.

privateAuthorNotes: A human reviewer must verify source fidelity and ensure the distractor remains plausible without teaching it as fact.

options.text: Classify waiting versus computation and the desired latency or throughput; choose concurrency for overlapping progress and parallel resources only when simultaneous work offsets coordination cost.
Concurrency and parallelism mean the same thing, and adding threads always makes any Python workload faster.
